name: NouPro App Logic
description: Complete application logic, business rules, and data structures for the NouPro B2B distribution management platform. This serves as the single source of truth for AI-assisted development.

product.name: NouPro
product.vision: Give professionals a powerful tool to have full control over their products, orders, and business operations.
product.positioning: The modern distribution tool built for Mauritius.
product.targetMarket: Small distributors and retailers in Mauritius
product.longTermVision: The professional platform that powers the backbone of small business operations - from Mauritius to Africa to global niches.

problemStatement.core: Small distributors and retailers still depend heavily on paper-based workflows for ordering, stock management, invoicing, and product tracking, causing inefficiencies, errors, and operational delays.
painPoints: Data duplication (write on paper → rewrite into software)
No backup of important business information

primary.description: Businesses that need to manage products, stocks, orders, invoicing, and deliveries in one place

usageContext: On mobile devices for quick actions
Daily usage for orders, invoices, and stock checks
Weekly usage for analytics and reports

identitySystem.description: NouPro operates on a two-layer identity model: Personal Profile and Business Profile

profileTypes.description: There are exactly TWO types of profiles in the app

userProfile.description: Personal profile representing an individual user. REQUIRED for everyone to use the app.
userProfile.requirement: Every user MUST have exactly ONE User Profile to access the app
purpose: Switching to Business Profiles they own or have access to
Being added to staff lists by businesses
userProfile.importance: Ensures one user can manage or join multiple businesses. Staff never log in 'as the business'. A user remains an individual first, then activates business identities as needed.

businessProfile.description: Profile of a company/professional entity. Shows products that can be ordered by others in business mode.
businessProfile.visibleTo: Anyone can view business profiles, but only users in Business Mode can order products
businessProfile.keyPrinciple: A retailer is simply a business profile (no paid subscription plan) with fewer tools. No special 'retailer accounts' needed.

profileViewingModes.description: How profiles appear when viewed by others vs. when viewing your own

viewingOthersProfile.description: When viewing someone else's profile (User or Business)

viewingOwnUserProfile.name: Personal User Profile Screen (User Mode)
viewingOwnUserProfile.description: When viewing your OWN User Profile. Shows the same layout as others see.

viewingOwnBusinessProfile.name: Personal Business Profile Screen (Business Mode)
viewingOwnBusinessProfile.description: When viewing your OWN Business Profile. Shows the same layout as others see.

staffAccess.description: A User Profile linked to one or more Business Profiles with specific roles
staffAccess.loginMethod: Staff always log in with their User Profile, never 'as the business'
staffAccess.accessMethod: Gain access to specific Business Profiles through permissions
staffAccess.workflow: Switch into the business identity to perform tasks

profileSwitching.description: Switching profile = switching identity, not logging out
profileSwitching.userModeActive: User acts as an individual (personal / Staff)
profileSwitching.businessModeActive: User acts as the company (distributor, retailer, service, etc.)
profileSwitching.uiChange: Tabs, permissions, and UI instantly change based on active profile

navigation.description: Bottom tabs depend entirely on the active profile type. Inbox and Notifications are accessed via overlays from the Home header, not as tabs.

personalProfileTabs.description: For individuals browsing, chatting, and ordering

tabs.name: Home
tabs.description: Feed & updates from businesses, suggestions. Header contains Notifications and Inbox icons with badges. Swipe left opens Inbox overlay.

tabs.name: Explore
tabs.description: Discover businesses, browse categories, search businesses/products

tabs.name: Activity
tabs.description: All orders placed as a person (status, history, notifications)

tabs.name: Profile
tabs.description: Personal settings, and entry to switch or manage business profiles

overlays.description: Screens accessible from Home header, slide from right

businessProfileTabs.description: For businesses performing operational tasks (Pro Mode)

tabs.name: Home
tabs.description: Business dashboard with KPI chips, quick actions, priority queue, and activity timeline. Header contains Notifications and Inbox icons with badges. Swipe left opens Inbox overlay.

tabs.name: Deliveries
tabs.description: Assign, track and create deliveries and transfers, Incoming orders, manual order creation, order status

tabs.name: Products
tabs.description: Product catalog management, Stock levels, adjustments, low stock alerts

tabs.name: Invoices
tabs.description: Invoices, estimates, invoices status, invoices history, invoices details

tabs.name: Business profile
tabs.description: Business profile with the cover, profile picture, Descriptions, products and about us, Business settings, staff, subscription, public page controls

overlays.description: Screens accessible from Home header, slide from right

staffViewNavigation.description: Staff can sees all tabs but can't have access to detailed informations

businessToBusiness.description: A business (retailer or another distributor) can order from another business

orderingSystem.personalProfileOrdering: Personal profiles CANNOT place orders. Everyone who wants to order needs to create a business account.

roles.description: Three-tier role system within business profiles

admin.description: Manager with most business operations access
capabilities: Limited staff management (invite and remove staff)
Access only permitted tabs based on role assignment

paywallTriggers.description: Actions that require upgrade from Free plan

triggers.behavior: Show upgrade modal: needs Paid plan
Block — inform user that orders require Paid plan

triggers.action: Exceed staff limit on paid tier
triggers.behavior: Prompt to move to higher tier

permissionsMatrix.description: Complete permissions by profile type, role, and plan

feedPosts.description: Feed contains three types of posts: brand_presentation, company_presentation, new_products

brand_presentation.description: Shows a brand with products from a distributor

navigation.onBrandHeaderPress: Navigate to distributor's ViewBusinessProfile with expandBrandId to auto-expand that brand's products
navigation.onProductCardPress: Navigate to ProductDetailPublic with productId and businessId

company_presentation.description: Shows a company/distributor with their brands

navigation.onBrandCardPress: Navigate to company's ViewBusinessProfile with expandBrandId to auto-expand that brand's products

new_products.description: Shows new products added or received by a business

navigation.onProductCardPress: Navigate to ProductDetailPublic with productId and businessId

productDetailPublicScreen.description: Public view of a product from feed
features: Full-screen product image with NEW badge if applicable
Brand/business info with link to ViewBusinessProfile

explore.action: Opens Public Business Profiles when a business is tapped

dashboard.description: Personal activity overview replacing Inbox tab
dashboard.purpose: Quick stats and recent activity for the user

content.recentActivity: List of recent orders, deliveries, connections

inboxOverlay.description: Thread list → Chat view (overlay, not tab)
inboxOverlay.behavior: Slides from right, hides tab bar

notificationsOverlay.behavior: Slides from right, hides tab bar

activity.description: List of personal tasks. When an admin put a staff in a delivery task, it will appear here
activity.scope: Only tasks placed as personal profile

profile.name: Personal User Profile Screen (User Mode)
profile.description: Shows your own User Profile with the same layout others see, but with Edit/Share buttons instead of Message/Connect

profileSwitcher.description: Allows user to switch between User Profile and Business Profiles
profileSwitcher.location: Top of screen, tappable area showing current profile
profileSwitcher.behavior: Opens profile selection modal/dropdown with all available profiles

personal.description: Always visible - shows Personal Profile option

businesses.description: Only shown when user has at least one business in their list
businesses.showCondition: Shown after user creates or joins their first business

sections.name: Privacy Policy
items: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service

sections.name: Community
items: A place where users can propose new features and report bugs

home.name: Pro Home / Business Dashboard
home.description: Business-operating dashboard that reduces time-to-action for Orders, Deliveries, Products/Stock, Invoices, Messages

kpiChipsRow.description: Horizontal scrollable chips showing today's metrics
kpiChipsRow.behavior: Tap chip → navigates to relevant filtered list

activityTimeline.description: Lightweight timeline of recent business activity (operational, not social)

displayBehavior.showMostRecent: Display only the 5 most recent activity items

emptyState.condition: When business has no activity (new business or no recorded activity)
emptyState.subMessage: Your recent business activity will appear here

postsSection.description: Feed posts like Personal mode, displayed in white container
postsSection.behavior: Scrolls up to reveal info section above

feedNavigation.description: Same navigation as Personal mode - product cards → ProductDetailPublic, brand cards → ViewBusinessProfile with expandBrandId

mvpConstraints: All blocks must support: Empty state, Loading skeleton, Error fallback
Posts section should have white background contrasting with light grey page background

inboxOverlay.description: Thread list → Chat view (overlay, not tab)
inboxOverlay.purpose: Messages between the business and clients/partners
inboxOverlay.behavior: Slides from right, hides tab bar

notificationsOverlay.behavior: Slides from right, hides tab bar

elements: View selector for All products, My products, Imported products, Display products

screens.Search bar: Search for a product by name or brand
screens.Location selector: Select a location to view products (shows only own business locations, auto-selects if single location - see locationsAndModesMVP.locationSelectionUI)
screens.Status selector: Select a status to filter products
screens.Category selector: Select a category to filter products

screens.name: Product List
screens.description: List/grid with product name, price, stock status, publish status

screens.name: Edit Product
screens.description: Same as Add, populated with current data

screens.name: Product Details
screens.description: Full details, images, stock history link (future)

screens.name: Stock Overview
screens.description: List of products with quantities, low-stock badges

screens.name: Stock History (later)
screens.description: Timeline of stock adjustments per product

screens.name: Create Order
flow: Choose existing customer or create new

productChecklist.description: Table showing products to be loaded for delivery with checkboxes

viewingOthersBusinessProfile.name: Business Profile (Viewing Others)
viewingOthersBusinessProfile.description: When viewing another company's Business Profile - can browse products and order (if in Business Mode)

tabs.name: Products
tabs.description: Published products grid/list, tap = product detail, add to Cart (only in Business Mode)

tabs.name: Cart
tabs.note: Cart and ordering only available when viewing from Business Mode

viewingOwnBusinessProfile.name: Personal Business Profile Screen (Business Mode)
viewingOwnBusinessProfile.description: When viewing your OWN Business Profile - shows same layout as others see but with Edit/Share buttons

profileSwitcher.description: Allows user to switch between User Profile and Business Profiles
profileSwitcher.location: Top of screen, tappable area showing current business profile
profileSwitcher.behavior: Opens profile selection modal/dropdown with all available profiles

personal.description: Always visible - shows Personal Profile option

businesses.description: Only shown when user has at least one business in their list
businesses.showCondition: Shown after user creates or joins their first business

tabs.name: Products
tabs.description: All products (published and unpublished), tap = product detail/edit

viewingOwnBusinessProfile.note: No Cart tab when viewing own profile - this is management view, not ordering view

viewingOthersUserProfile.name: User Profile (Viewing Others)
viewingOthersUserProfile.description: When viewing another user's profile - can see their info and message them

flow0_feedProductDiscovery.name: Feed Product Discovery and Ordering
flow0_feedProductDiscovery.goal: Discover products from feed and view/order them
steps: View feed on Home screen
See brand_presentation, company_presentation, or new_products posts
Tap on product card → navigate to ProductDetailPublic screen
View product details (image, name, unit, price, brand info)
Adjust quantity and tap 'Add to Order'
Alternatively: Tap brand/business header → navigate to ViewBusinessProfile to see all products

flow0b_feedBusinessDiscovery.name: Feed Business Discovery
flow0b_feedBusinessDiscovery.goal: Discover businesses and their product catalogs from feed
steps: View feed on Home screen
See company_presentation or brand_presentation post
Tap on company/brand header → navigate to ViewBusinessProfile
View business profile with all products
Browse products, add to cart, send order request

flow1_distributorProductToDelivery.name: Distributor: Product to Delivery
flow1_distributorProductToDelivery.goal: Full lifecycle from product creation to delivery
steps: Switch to Business Profile
Products → Add Product → fill details → Save
Stock → select product → add stock quantity
Customer (another Business) places order OR distributor creates it manually
Orders → view incoming order → review → Accept
Deliveries → assign delivery staff → update status until Delivered

flow2_retailerOrdersFromDistributor.name: Retailer Orders from Distributor
flow2_retailerOrdersFromDistributor.goal: Place an order to another business
steps: Switch to Retailer's Business Profile
Go to Explore / search
Open Distributor Public Business Page
Browse Products tab → add items to Cart
Go to Cart → confirm details → submit Order Request
Order appears in Distributor's Orders tab

flow3_manualOrderCreation.name: Distributor Manually Creates Order
flow3_manualOrderCreation.goal: Create order for retailer from a call or offline request
steps: Business Profile → Orders tab → 'Create Order'
Select existing Retailer (Business) or create new Business
Add products from catalog, set quantities
Review summary → Confirm
Optionally, Deliveries → create and assign delivery for that order

flow4_staffDeliveryFlow.name: Staff Delivery Flow
flow4_staffDeliveryFlow.goal: Only see and update their own deliveries
steps: Log in → Personal Profile Home
Switch to assigned Business Profile
In business mode, only Deliveries tab is visible
Open Delivery List → tap assigned delivery
Update status: Pending → On the way → Delivered
Optionally add note/photo as proof

flow5_businessSetupAndPublish.name: Business Setup & Public Profile Publishing
flow5_businessSetupAndPublish.goal: Create business, configure and publish public page
steps: From Personal Profile → 'Create Business Profile'
Enter company info: name, logo, description, industry
Switch into new Business Profile
Go to Business tab → Public Profile section
Toggle 'Publish Profile' ON
Select which products are public
Save & preview public page

feedDataSource.description: Feed posts are fetched from the backend API with pagination

parameters.limit: Number of posts to fetch (default: 20, max: 50)

brand_presentation.description: Shows a brand with multiple products from a distributor

data.products: Array of products with id, name, unit, price, image, isNew

company_presentation.description: Shows a company/distributor with their available brands

data.isConnected: Boolean - whether current user is connected to this business
data.brands: Array of brands with id, name, logo, productsCount

new_products.description: Shows new products added by a distributor or received by a business

data.products: Array of products with id, name, unit, price, image, brandName

productDetailEndpoint.description: Fetches single product details for ProductDetailPublic screen
productDetailEndpoint.response: Product object with brand/business info attached

locationsAndModesMVP.description: Multi-location architecture with DEPENDENT/INDEPENDENT operating modes

DEPENDENT.description: Default mode for all locations. Orders/invoices created by Parent Business only.

INDEPENDENT.description: Location operates as semi-autonomous entity with its own public page and order creation.
capabilities: Uses Parent Product Master (can override price/tax if allowed)
INDEPENDENT.requirement: Only available with enterprise tier subscription

downgradePolicy.description: How the system handles tier downgrades (e.g., enterprise → business)
downgradePolicy.principle: Preserve data, block capabilities at runtime

dataPreservation: Independent locations remain stored as operatingMode=INDEPENDENT
location.isPublic stays true in data (not overwritten)
All location data preserved for instant re-enable on upgrade
capabilityBlocking: Public storefront routes return PLAN_REQUIRED (403), not 404
Direct ordering at location level blocked (PLAN_REQUIRED)
Direct invoicing at location level blocked (PLAN_REQUIRED)
New independent locations cannot be created
Switching INDEPENDENT → DEPENDENT is always allowed (one-way)
uiGuidance: Use publicEffective boolean for real display state
Show publicDisabledReason when isPublic=true but publicEffective=false
Location settings: disable toggle, show upgrade CTA
Customer deep links: show 'Storefront unavailable' page

computedFields.publicEffective: isLocationPublicEffective(business, location) - true only if INDEPENDENT + isPublic + Enterprise
computedFields.publicDisabledReason: getPublicDisabledReason(business, location) - null or 'Enterprise plan required'

capabilityDerivation.description: Capabilities are derived from subscriptionTier, not stored directly
capabilityDerivation.principle: Single source of truth - never sprinkle 'if tier ===' checks
capabilityDerivation.enforcement: Server enforces capabilities on all API endpoints

LOCATION.description: Order created by an INDEPENDENT location
LOCATION.soldByLocationId: The location that created the order

LOCATION.description: Invoice issued by an INDEPENDENT location
LOCATION.issuedByLocationId: The location that issued the invoice

ASSIGNED.description: Assigned to a location for fulfillment

locationSelectionUI.description: Location dropdown behavior in main screens - shows only own business locations
locationSelectionUI.principle: Users can only select from locations belonging to their own business

persistence.description: Selected location is persisted across app sessions
behavior: When app opens, restores last selected location
If 'All Locations' was selected (null), shows 'All Locations'
If specific location was selected, restores that selection
If persisted location no longer exists, defaults to 'All Locations'

dataSource.note: IMPORTANT: Screens must use 'locations' from businessStore, NOT currentCompany.locations
dataSource.defensiveCheck: Always use: const safeLocations = Array.isArray(locations) ? locations : []

singleLocation.description: Business has only one location (the main/primary location)
rules: Auto-selects the primary location on mount via useEffect
Hides 'All Locations' option from dropdown
Disables the dropdown touch handler
Hides chevron icon (no dropdown indicator)
Displays only the location name
User cannot change selection - data is filtered to that location

multipleLocations.description: Business has more than one location
rules: Shows 'All Locations' option at the top of dropdown
Lists all business locations below 'All Locations'
Dropdown is interactive with chevron icon visible
User can switch between any location or 'All Locations'
Data is filtered based on selected location (or shows all if 'All Locations')

storeArchitecture.description: businessStore manages locations fetching and state

importAliasing.critical: API functions MUST be aliased to avoid Zustand getter shadowing
importAliasing.correct: import { get as apiGet, put as apiPut, post as apiPost, del as apiDel } from '@/shared/services/api';
importAliasing.reason: Zustand's create((set, get) => ...) provides a 'get' function that shadows the API import

fetchFlow: 2. On success/mock fallback, setBusiness(businesses[0]) is called

steps: 1. App mounts, CompanyStoreInitializer renders
2. useEffect calls fetchCompanies() on mount
3. fetchCompanies() → setBusiness() → fetchLocations(businessId)
4. businessStore.locations is populated with business locations
5. If only one location, auto-select it as currentLocation
6. Screens read from useBusinessStore().locations

mockData.description: When API is unavailable, mock data is used
mockData.note: Mock data should be consistent between businessStore and LocationsScreen

businesses.GET /api/businesses/:businessId: Get business with capabilities and locations

locations.GET /api/locations/:locationId: Get single location with business info

locationSwitcher.description: Business → Location switcher in app header
behavior: On login, fetch user's businesses and locations
Store activeBusinessId and activeLocationId in state

lockedModeUI.description: When business cannot change location modes (non-BUSINESS tier)
lockedModeUI.behavior: Show mode selector as disabled with upgrade CTA
lockedModeUI.message: Upgrade to Business tier to enable Independent Locations

user.description: Personal Profile - every user has one

business.description: Business Profile - professional entity with subscription tier and capabilities

allowLocationPriceOverride.description: Allow locations to override product prices

allowLocationTaxOverride.description: Allow locations to override tax rates

location.description: Physical location of a business with operating mode

operatingMode.description: INDEPENDENT only allowed with BUSINESS tier

businessMember.description: Links users to businesses with roles and permissions

priceOverride.description: Location-specific price, null uses business default

taxOverride.description: Location-specific tax rate, null uses business default

brand.description: Product brands owned by a business

is_published.description: Shown on public profile if true

order.description: B2B orders with scope (PARENT or LOCATION) and fulfillment assignment

soldByScope.description: PARENT = created at business level, LOCATION = created by independent location

soldByLocationId.description: Location that created order (only for LOCATION scope)

fulfillmentLocationId.description: Location assigned to fulfill this order

invoiceDocument.description: Invoices and estimates with scope (PARENT or LOCATION)

issuedByScope.description: PARENT = issued at business level, LOCATION = issued by independent location

issuedByLocationId.description: Location that issued invoice (only for LOCATION scope)

descriptions.ASSIGNED: Assigned to a location for fulfillment

descriptions.DEPENDENT: Fulfills parent orders only, no own orders/invoices
descriptions.INDEPENDENT: Own orders, invoices, and public storefront (enterprise tier only)

languages.notes: English is default business language, French widely spoken

included: Event/System messages (conversation start, user removed, name change)

keyPrinciples: Personal Profile = individual user, NO business tools
Business Profile = professional entity with full operational tools
Profile switching = identity change, not logout
Users always logs in as Personal first, then switches to Business
Free plan = internal management only, no public features
Paid plans = unlock selling, deliveries, invoices, public page
uiConsistency: Use design.json for all UI tokens (colors, typography, spacing)
Use theme/index.ts for runtime theme values
Follow established component patterns (Cards, Pills, Buttons)
Maintain consistent navigation based on active profile type
Use settingsListItem pattern for all settings/option list screens
Use modalListItem pattern for all modal/bottom sheet lists

listStylingGuidelines.description: Standard list item styling for consistency across the app
principles: Border lines should NOT touch screen edges - use marginHorizontal: 12
Use 0.5 border width for subtle separators
Icon size 24 for list items, chevron size 20
Consistent height of 60 for touch targets
SemiBold font for titles in list items

stateManagement: Track active profile type (personal vs business)
Track current business ID when in business mode
Track user role/permissions for current business
Track subscription plan for paywall logic

confirmationDialogs.description: Use AppModal component instead of Alert.alert for all confirmation and success feedback

delete.when: Destructive actions that cannot be undone

confirm.when: Asking user to confirm BEFORE performing an action

success.when: Providing feedback AFTER an action completed successfully

rules: NEVER use native Alert.alert for success messages - use AppModal with 'success' variant
NEVER show checkmark icon when ASKING for confirmation - only when showing SUCCESS
Use 'delete' variant for any destructive action confirmation
Use 'confirm' variant when asking 'Would you like to...?' questions
Use 'success' variant when showing 'Successfully completed!' messages

projectArchitecture.description: Production-grade feature-based architecture for NouPro React Native app

folderStructure.description: Three-layer architecture: modes, features, and shared modules

modes.description: Mode-specific screens and configuration (Personal vs Business)
modes.principle: Modes are NOT features - they define navigation context and access patterns

personal.config: Tab configuration, access rules, home screen definition

business.config: Tab configuration, access rules, feature gating

features.description: Domain features - pure business logic modules
features.principle: Each feature is self-contained with its own screens and components

modules.name: inbox
modules.description: Messaging and chat functionality (V1 Light: text messages and PDFs from invoices only, no attachments/voice)

chatScreen.description: Full chat interface with message bubbles, input, and special message types

text.description: Regular text message with link detection
text.linkHighlighting: blue for incoming (#2563EB), light blue for outgoing (#93C5FD)

pdf.tapAction: Opens document preview with download/share options

event.description: System event messages (centered, light gray background, italic). NO date/timestamp shown - Date Separator is rendered separately above.
event.relationship: Date Separator always appears ABOVE Event/System messages visually

order.description: Order card with status, payment info, and action buttons

invoice.description: Invoice card with ID and tap-to-view

order_event.description: Order Event Card - accordion-based order summary card

expandedContent.description: List of items with individual prices
Payment activity timeline showing partial or full payments

messageTypes.v1NotSupportedPlaceholder: Shows grayed out placeholder with 'Not available in this version'

dateSeparators.description: WhatsApp-style date headers between messages from different days. ALWAYS appears ABOVE Event/System messages on the same date. Separate from Event/System messages.

formatting.overOneWeek: Short day + full date (e.g., Mon, 23 Jan 2025)

dateSeparators.orderWithEvents: Date Separator always renders BEFORE Event/System messages visually (above in inverted list)

timestamps.description: Message timestamps show time only (HH:MM), no date

messageStatus.description: Status indicators for outgoing messages only

orderCardDesign.description: Special bubble design for order messages

eventBubbleDesign.description: Centered system event messages. NO timestamp/date - Date Separator is handled separately and always appears ABOVE event messages.
eventBubbleDesign.separationFromDateSeparator: Event/System messages are visually distinct from Date Separators - different background color, border radius, font style

deleteMessage.description: Long press to delete message functionality
deleteMessage.confirmation: Alert with Cancel and Delete Message options

inputArea.description: V1 simplified input (text only, no attachments)

v1Limitations.description: V1 Light version has simplified chat functionality

shared.description: Shared modules used across features and modes

ui.description: UI primitives ONLY - reusable atomic components with no domain knowledge
ui.note: Headers and search composites moved to layout/ and features/search/

layout.description: Layout components - headers, shells, screen wrappers

files.PersonalTabNavigator.tsx: Bottom tabs for Personal mode (Home, Explore, Inbox, Activity, Profile)
files.BusinessTabNavigator.tsx: Bottom tabs for Business mode (Inbox, Deliveries, Products, Invoices, Business)

importConventions.description: Standardized import paths using TypeScript path aliases
rules: Always use @/ alias instead of relative paths
Import from feature index when importing multiple items from same feature
UI primitives come from @/shared/components/ui/
Layout components (headers) come from @/shared/components/layout/headers/
Search composites come from @/features/search/components/
Feature-specific components come from @/features/{feature}/components/

modesVsFeatures.description: Critical distinction between modes and features

modes.definition: Navigation contexts that define what tabs/screens a user sees
modes.contains: Mode-specific screens, tab configs, access rules

features.definition: Self-contained domain modules with business logic
features.contains: Domain screens, domain components, feature-specific logic

criteria: Used within the feature or closely related features

navigationArchitecture.description: Dual-mode navigation based on active profile type. Inbox and Notifications are overlays, not tabs.

PersonalTabNavigator.note: Inbox removed from tabs, now an overlay from Home

BusinessTabNavigator.note: Home added as first tab, Inbox removed (now an overlay)

overlays.description: Screens that slide from right and hide the tab bar
overlays.accessedVia: Home header icons or swipe left gesture

modeSwitching.uiChange: Tabs, permissions, and available screens change instantly
